name: A-1 Skyraider
prompt:
Vietnam War era Douglas A-1 Skyraider "Sandy" / "Spad". Piston-engine attack aircraft used for close air support and rescue escort.

Physical structure:
- Fuselage: large single-engine prop plane, ~11.8m long
  - Massive radial engine cowling at front (Wright R-3350)
  - Large 4-blade propeller
  - Single-seat cockpit with bubble canopy
  - Fuselage tapers to tail
  - Dive brakes on fuselage sides (show closed)
- Wings: large straight low-mounted wings
  - ~15.2m span
  - 15 hardpoints total! (show 8 with ordnance)
  - Wing-fold capability (show extended)
  - Each wing thick, with rounded tips
- Tail: conventional tail with single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer
  - Rudder on vertical stab
  - Elevators on horizontal stab
- Landing gear: taildragger configuration
  - Two main gear with large wheels under wings (or retracted)
  - Tail wheel
- Ordnance: massive weapons load
  - Mix of bombs, rocket pods, napalm canisters on wing pylons
  - Show 4 bombs and 2 rocket pods

Colors: SEA camo - OD green (0x556B2F) and tan (0x8B7355), gray undersides (0xaaaaaa), dark prop (0x333333), engine cowl (0x444444), canopy (0x6688aa)
Orientation: On ground or flight pose, facing +Z, propeller at front
Scale: Real-world meters (~11.8m long, ~15.2m wingspan)`,
    animations: `Propeller spin: 4-blade prop on Z axis, 0.3s per revolution`
  },
  {
    name: 'M48 Patton Tank',
    slug: 'm48-patton',
    category: 'vehicles/ground',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 8000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era M48A3 Patton medium tank. Primary US Army tank used in Vietnam.

Physical structure:
- Hull: cast/welded steel hull with distinctive rounded shape
  - Glacis plate: rounded front armor
  - Hull sides: vertical with slight tumblehome (inward angle at top)
  - Rear: engine deck with grilles
  - Driver's hatch: front center of hull top
  - Hull length ~6.9m
- Turret: large hemispherical cast turret
  - Commander's cupola: raised cupola on top right with M2 .50 cal
  - Loader's hatch: on top left
  - Mantlet: thick curved gun mount at front of turret
  - Bustle: rear overhang of turret for radio equipment
  - Searchlight: infrared searchlight above main gun
- Main gun: 90mm M41 gun, ~4.5m barrel
  - Bore evacuator (bulge) near muzzle
  - Muzzle brake (T-shaped)
- Track system:
  - 6 road wheels per side
  - Drive sprocket at rear
  - Idler wheel at front
  - Return rollers on top
  - Wide tracks with chevron pattern (simplified as box)
  - Side skirts: none (tracks visible)
- Exhaust: rear, large grille with exhaust port
- Fenders: flat fenders over tracks with stowage

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark tracks (0x2a2a2a), gun steel (0x3a3a3a), road wheels (0x444444), searchlight lens (0xffffcc)
Orientation: Hull facing +Z, sitting on tracks at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~6.9m long, ~3.6m wide, ~3.1m tall)`,
    animations: `Turret traverse: turret rotates Y-axis 45 degrees left then right, 6s cycle
Gun elevation: gun barrel tilts up 10 degrees then down 5 degrees, 4s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'PT-76 Amphibious Tank (NVA)',
    slug: 'pt76',
    category: 'vehicles/ground',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 5000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Soviet PT-76 amphibious light tank. Used by NVA, notably at Battle of Lang Vei.

Physical structure:
- Hull: flat-bottomed boat-shaped hull for amphibious ops
  - Low profile, wide and flat
  - Bow: pointed, with trim vane (folding plate at very front for water ops, shown raised at 45 degrees)
  - Flat deck, very low freeboard
  - Rear: water jet outlets, engine grilles
  - Length ~6.9m, width ~3.1m
- Turret: small rounded turret, offset slightly forward
  - Flattened dome shape
  - Commander's hatch on top
- Main gun: 76mm D-56T gun, medium length barrel
  - No muzzle brake
  - Bore evacuator near midpoint
  - Coaxial MG mount beside main gun
- Track system:
  - 6 road wheels per side (large diameter, no return rollers)
  - Drive sprocket at rear
  - Idler at front
  - Wide tracks for soft ground/water traction
- Very low silhouette overall (~2.3m tall)

Colors: Dark military green (0x3d4a2a), darker tracks (0x2a2a2a), gun metal (0x3a3a3a), trim vane (0x4a5a3a)
Orientation: Hull facing +Z, on tracks at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~6.9m long, ~3.1m wide, ~2.3m tall)`,
    animations: `Turret traverse: Y-axis rotation +/-30 degrees, 5s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Tiger',
    slug: 'tiger',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'character',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Indochinese tiger (Panthera tigris corbetti). Apex predator of Vietnam's jungles, feared by soldiers on all sides.

Physical structure (low-poly, built from primitives):
- Body: elongated powerful torso
  - Chest area wider, tapering toward hips
  - ~2m body length
  - Use elongated box or capsule shape
- Head: rounded, broad head
  - Short muzzle/snout
  - Small rounded ears on top
  - Whisker area (simplified)
  - Open mouth: lower jaw slightly dropped showing teeth (optional)
- Legs: 4 powerful legs
  - Front legs: thick, straight
  - Rear legs: slightly angled at hock
  - Large paws (flat boxes)
  - ~0.8m tall legs
- Tail: long, thick at base, tapering
  - ~1m long, slight curve
  - Striped pattern (use multiple short segments with alternating colors)
- Overall: powerful, muscular, low-slung predatory stance

Colors: Orange body (0xcc6622), white belly/chest (0xe8dcc8), black stripes (build separate parts for stripes or use darker material patches), black nose/ears (0x222222), green eyes (0x44aa22)
Note: Stripes can be simplified as dark box strips on body surface (offset 0.01 to avoid z-fighting)
Orientation: Standing, facing +Z, legs on ground at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~2.5m nose to tail tip, ~0.9m tall at shoulder)`,
    animations: `Walk cycle: legs move in alternating pairs, 2s cycle
Tail sway: tail pivot rotates Z-axis +/-15 degrees, 1.5s cycle
Head bob: head pivot slight Y rotation +/-5 degrees, 3s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'King Cobra Snake',
    slug: 'king-cobra',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'prop',
    budget: 500,
    prompt: `King Cobra (Ophiophagus hannah). Largest venomous snake in Vietnam, up to 5m. Show in defensive hood-spread pose.

Physical structure:
- Body coils: build from several cylinder/capsule segments arranged in S-curve on ground
  - ~3m total visible body, ~0.06m diameter
  - 3-4 segments creating gentle curves on ground
- Raised head/neck: front portion raised ~0.5m off ground
  - Neck section raised vertically, slight S-curve
- Hood: distinctive spread cobra hood behind head
  - Flattened oval shape, wider than body (~0.2m wide)
  - Use flattened box or scaled cylinder
- Head: triangular/elongated head
  - Slightly wider than neck
  - Small eyes on sides
  - Snout pointed

Colors: Olive-brown body (0x5a5030), lighter belly (0x8a7a5a), darker hood markings (0x3a3020), dark eyes (0x111111)
Orientation: Coiled on ground at Y=0, raised head facing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~3m visible body length, ~0.5m raised head height)`,
    animations: `Hood sway: raised head/neck pivot sways Y-axis +/-10 degrees, 2s cycle
Body breathing: subtle scale on raised section, 3s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Wild Boar',
    slug: 'wild-boar',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'character',
    budget: 2000,
    prompt: `Vietnamese wild boar (Sus scrofa). Common in Vietnam's forests, aggressive when cornered. Known hazard for patrols.

Physical structure:
- Body: barrel-shaped, heavy, compact torso
  - ~1.2m long, ~0.5m wide
  - High shoulders, lower rump (slight downward slope)
  - Coarse, bristly appearance
- Head: large wedge-shaped head
  - Long snout/muzzle with flat disk-like nose at end
  - Small eyes on sides
  - Pointed ears, upright
  - Tusks: two curved tusks (short cones) protruding from lower jaw upward
  - Jaw heavy and powerful
- Legs: 4 short but sturdy legs
  - Hooved feet (dark)
  - Relatively short for body size
- Tail: short, thin, curly tail
- Overall: stocky, muscular, low to ground, aggressive stance

Colors: Dark bristly brown (0x4a3a28), lighter snout (0x7a6a55), pink inner ears (0x8a6a6a), dark hooves (0x1a1a1a), ivory tusks (0xddd8c8)
Orientation: Standing, facing +Z, on ground at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~1.5m nose to tail, ~0.8m tall at shoulder)`,
    animations: `Idle breathing: body bob 0.02m, 2s cycle
Head rooting: head pivot nods down 15 degrees and up, 2s cycle (sniffing ground)`
  },
  {
    name: 'Bird Flock (Egrets)',
    slug: 'bird-flock',
    category: 'animals',
    assetCategory: 'prop',
    budget: 500,
    prompt: `Flock of 5 white egrets (cattle egrets) in flight formation. Common sight over Vietnam rice paddies and waterways.

Physical structure (5 very simple birds):
- Each bird (budget ~100 tris each):
  - Body: small elongated capsule or box, ~0.15m long
  - Wings: two flat thin boxes angled outward and slightly back, wingspan ~0.4m
    - Wings slightly angled up from body (dihedral)
  - Head: tiny sphere on front, ~0.03m
  - Neck: thin short cylinder connecting head to body
  - Tail: tiny thin flat box at rear
  - Beak: tiny cone pointing forward from head
  - Legs: omitted (tucked in flight) or tiny thin cylinders trailing behind
- Flock arrangement: V-formation or loose cluster
  - Bird 1 (lead): center, slightly forward
  - Birds 2-3: flanking left and right, slightly behind and offset
  - Birds 4-5: further back, wider spread
  - Vertical variation: slight height differences (0.1-0.3m apart in Y)

Colors: White body/wings (0xf0f0f0), yellow beak (0xddaa33), dark wing tips (0xcccccc), legs (0x444444)
Orientation: Flying toward +Z, flock center at ~2m height (Y=2)
Scale: Real-world proportioned but each bird simplified (~0.4m wingspan)`,
    animations: `Wing flap: all wings animate up/down, 1s cycle (position track on wing pivots)
Flock bob: root node gentle sine wave position, 3s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'SA-2 Guideline SAM',
    slug: 'sa2-sam',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 3000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Soviet SA-2 Guideline (S-75 Dvina) surface-to-air missile on launch rail. The "flying telephone pole" that shot down US aircraft over North Vietnam.

Physical structure:
- Missile (~10.7m total length):
  - Booster: large cylindrical rear section with 4 large fins
    - ~0.65m diameter, ~3m long
    - 4 delta fins at rear (cruciform arrangement), ~0.5m span each
  - Sustainer: slimmer main body section
    - ~0.5m diameter, ~6m long
    - 4 smaller control fins at mid-body (cruciform)
    - Nose cone: pointed ogive shape
  - Junction: slight diameter change between booster and sustainer
- Launch rail: angled steel beam rail
  - ~12m long, set at ~45 degree elevation angle
  - Rail beam: box or I-beam shape
  - Missile sits on rail, held by cradle/shoes
- Pedestal: rotating pedestal base at ground level
  - Circular base, ~2m diameter
  - Elevation mechanism: hydraulic cylinder (simplified)
  - Turntable for azimuth
- Support structure: steel framework connecting rail to pedestal
  - Truss/frame structure

Colors: Missile body: white/cream (0xe8e0d0), green nose/fins (0x556B2F), booster tan (0xc8b878), rail dark steel (0x444444), pedestal (0x555555)
Orientation: Missile pointing up at ~45 degrees toward +Z, base on ground at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~10.7m missile, ~3m pedestal height, whole assembly ~8m tall)`,
    animations: `Launcher elevation: rail tilts from 15 to 75 degrees over 4 seconds, then back`
  },
  {
    name: 'Wooden Footbridge',
    slug: 'footbridge',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 2000,
    prompt: `Vietnamese wooden footbridge. Simple bridge spanning a stream or canal, common in rural areas and jungle trails.

Physical structure:
- Deck: wooden plank walkway, ~4m long, ~1.2m wide
  - Individual plank boards (8-10 planks side by side, each ~0.15m wide, ~0.03m thick)
  - Slight sag in middle (arc downward)
  - Planks laid across cross-beams
- Cross-beams: 3-4 transverse support beams under deck
  - ~0.1m x 0.1m timber, spaced evenly
- Supports: two sets of support structures, one at each end
  - A-frame or X-braced wooden posts
  - 2 posts per side, ~1.5m tall from water to deck
  - Cross-braced with diagonal beams
  - Posts driven into stream bank
- Railings: simple rope or bamboo handrails on each side
  - 2 vertical posts per side (at quarter and three-quarter points)
  - Single horizontal rail connecting posts, ~0.8m above deck
  - Can be bamboo poles (~0.04m diameter)
- Approach: slight ramps at each end where deck meets ground

Colors: Aged/weathered wood (0x7B6B45), darker wet wood at water level (0x5a4a30), bamboo railing (0xb5a068), rope lashing (0x8a7a5a)
Orientation: Bridge spans along Z axis, deck surface at Y ~1.5m (above water), walkable surface
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m long, ~1.2m wide, ~2m total height from water to railing)
